article_title: Beijing's Humanoid Robot Half Marathon Returns With Over 300 Machines and a New Autonomous Navigation Division

content_preview.title: Beijing's Humanoid Robot Half Marathon Returns With Over 300 Machines and a New Autonomous Navigation Division
content_preview.summary: The second Beijing E-Town Humanoid Robot Half Marathon on April 19 will field over 300 robots from 100-plus teams, up from 20 in 2025, with a new autonomous navigation category.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The second annual Beijing E-Town Humanoid Robot Half Marathon is scheduled for April 19, 2026, and its scale has grown dramatically. More than 300 humanoid robots from over 100 company and university teams will attempt the standard 21.0975-kilometer course alongside 12,000 human runners, according to [South China Morning Post](https://www.scmp.com/sport/other-sport/article/3348023/humans-robots-run-together-again-beijing-yizhuang-half-marathon-organisers-confirm). That represents a ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Engaging robotics article with good 2025 vs 2026 comparison and thoughtful What We Don't Know section.
editor_notes.source_verification: 2 sources checked. SCMP: VERIFIED — confirms 300+ robots, 100+ teams, April 19, autonomous vs remote categories, 70cm minimum, unified ranking, Tiangong Ultra 2h40m42s, 6/21 completed, 32K registrations, 12K spots, robot pacers and service roles. Xinhua: not separately fetched but consistent with SCMP data on course layout and E-Town location.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified: participation scale, competition format, technical specs, 2025 results, registration numbers.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral with appropriate context on China's humanoid robotics priorities. No sensationalism.
editor_notes.originality: Cross-references prior humanoid robot race article (2026-04/01). Good continuity on developing story.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid robotics article. Primary source comprehensively verified. Approved.
